channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Thousands of 'No Kings' protests take place around the world
item.description: Thousands of protests took place around the country today, against the Trump administration. We hear from people who came out about why they were there.
item.content:encoded: Thousands of protests took place around the country today, against the Trump administration. We hear from people who came out about why they were there.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: Ethics committee finds Florida Democratic representative guilty of violations
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found Rep. Shelia Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of ethics violations after a rare public hearing on allegations of financial crimes.
item.content:encoded: The House Ethics Committee found Rep. Shelia Cherfilus-McCormick guilty of ethics violations after a rare public hearing on allegations of financial crimes.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels enter the fighting in the Middle East
item.description: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.
item.content:encoded: At least 15 U.S. military personnel were wounded in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia and Yemen's Houthi rebels entered the war with an attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: How will two landmark social media verdicts reshape how we use this technology?
item.description: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.content:encoded: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Unpacking the competing narratives about the Texas primaries
item.description: Andrew Schneider, Senior Political Reporter for Houston Public Media, explains how competing narratives about the Texas Senate race have emerged.
item.content:encoded: Andrew Schneider, Senior Political Reporter for Houston Public Media, explains how competing narratives about the Texas Senate race have emerged.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: What explains the timeless appeal of Sherlock Holmes?
item.description: What's behind the timeless appeal of the quintessential fictional detective, Sherlock Holmes, who's been around for 140 years? Host Adrian Ma speaks with expert Sherlockian, Otto Penzler.
item.content:encoded: What's behind the timeless appeal of the quintessential fictional detective, Sherlock Holmes, who's been around for 140 years? Host Adrian Ma speaks with expert Sherlockian, Otto Penzler.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Singer Charlie Puth discusses his latest release, 'Whatever's Clever'
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Charlie Puth about his new album 'Whatever's Clever.'
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Charlie Puth about his new album 'Whatever's Clever.'
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: A provocative new film takes a hard look at Israeli life during the Gaza war
item.description: The Israeli film Yes criticizes Israeli artists during the Gaza war, and stars one of Israel's most provocative fringe artists, Ariel Bronz.
item.content:encoded: The Israeli film Yes criticizes Israeli artists during the Gaza war, and stars one of Israel's most provocative fringe artists, Ariel Bronz.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Progress on DHS funding stalls as polarized public opinion becomes more entrenched
item.description: Public support for Democrats and Republicans has hardened in recent months, leaving an increasingly narrow sliver of undecided voters.
item.content:encoded: Public support for Democrats and Republicans has hardened in recent months, leaving an increasingly narrow sliver of undecided voters.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Hegseth blocked 2 Black and 2 female soldiers from promotions
item.description: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.content:encoded: Pete Hegseth has reportedly blocked the military promotion of six officers: all women or Black men, to become one-star generals.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence
